content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

Find a permutation‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† PPP of the integers 111 to NNN such that

Pi‚Č•(Pi+1‚ąíPi‚ąí1)2P_i \geq \left( P_{i+1} - P_{i-1} \right)^2Pi‚Äč‚Č•(Pi+1‚Äč‚ąíPi‚ąí1‚Äč)2holds for every index iii from 222 to N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1.

It can be proved that at least one solution exists.

If multiple solutions exist, print any of them.

‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† A permutation of the integers 111 to NNN is an array of length NNN that contains every integer from 111 to NNN.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN space-separated integers ‚ÄĒ the permutation PPP for which the above condition holds.


If multiple solutions exist, you may print any of them.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is no index iii between 222 and N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1, so the condition is automatically satisfied for any permutation of length 222.
Test case 333: It can be observed that:

P2=4‚Č•(1‚ąí3)2=4P_2 = 4 \geq (1 - 3)^2 = 4P2‚Äč=4‚Č•(1‚ąí3)2=4
P3=3‚Č•(4‚ąí5)2=1P_3 = 3 \geq (4 - 5)^2 = 1P3‚Äč=3‚Č•(4‚ąí5)2=1
P4=5‚Č•(3‚ąí2)2=1P_4 = 5 \geq (3 - 2)^2 = 1P4‚Äč=5‚Č•(3‚ąí2)2=1
